text:
CT CHEST/ABDOMEN/PELVIS WITH INTRAVENOUS CONTRAST

CLINICAL INDICATION: 64-year-old female with known stage IV clear cell renal cell carcinoma, status post right radical cytoreductive nephrectomy. Prior treatment with ipilimumab/nivolumab followed by disease progression. Currently on cabozantinib (dose reduced to 40 mg daily). Restaging examination to assess treatment response. Please compare with prior CT dated approximately 3 months ago (pre-cabozantinib initiation).

TECHNIQUE: Multidetector CT of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis was performed following the administration of 100 mL of Omnipaque 350 intravenous contrast material. Images were acquired in the portal venous phase with a dedicated hepatic arterial phase obtained through the upper abdomen. Coronal and sagittal multiplanar reformations were reviewed in conjunction with axial images. Dose modulation employed.

COMPARISON: CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast (prior restaging examination, approximately 3 months prior).

--- FINDINGS ---

CHEST:

Lungs and Airways: The airways are patent to the segmental level bilaterally. No new consolidation, ground-glass opacity, or airspace disease.

The previously documented pulmonary nodules are again seen with the following interval changes:

- Right lower lobe: The dominant nodule in the posterior right lower lobe, previously measuring 2.4 cm at its largest dimension on the pre-cabozantinib study, now measures approximately 1.9 cm in the same plane. There is mild reduction in the degree of internal heterogeneity compared to prior. No cavitation or new surrounding ground-glass.

- Left upper lobe: A nodule in the apicoposterior segment, previously 1.2 cm, now measures approximately 0.9 cm. Somewhat less conspicuous than on prior.

- Right upper lobe: A nodule in the posterior segment of the right upper lobe, previously 1.4 cm, now measures approximately 1.1 cm. Margination is similar to prior.

- Left lower lobe: A nodule in the superior segment, previously 1.1 cm, now measures approximately 0.9 cm. Soft tissue density, unchanged morphology.

- Right middle lobe: Two previously described small nodules in the right middle lobe remain stable in size (approximately 0.4 and 0.5 cm, not significantly changed). No new right middle lobe lesions.

No new pulmonary nodules are identified. No cavitary lesions. No new tree-in-bud opacities. No pleural effusions. No pneumothorax.

Mediastinum and Hila: Heart size is normal. No pericardial effusion. Mediastinal and hilar contours appear unremarkable. No pathologically enlarged mediastinal or hilar lymph nodes. Aorta and main pulmonary artery are normal in caliber. No evidence of pulmonary embolism on this portal venous phase acquisition (dedicated PE study not performed).

Chest Wall, Pleura, and Osseous Structures: No aggressive-appearing osseous lesions in the thoracic spine, ribs, sternum, or visible shoulder girdles. Mild degenerative changes in the visualized thoracic spine. No pleural thickening or nodularity.

ABDOMEN:

Liver: The liver is normal in size and configuration. Two previously identified lesions in the right hepatic lobe are again seen:
- Segment VI lesion, previously 0.8 cm, now measures approximately 0.8 cm — essentially unchanged. On the arterial phase, there is mild peripheral enhancement with washout on portal venous phase, pattern remaining consistent with prior characterization as suspicious for metastatic deposit.
- Segment V/VI lesion, previously 0.6 cm, remains at approximately 0.6 cm. Enhancement characteristics unchanged.

No new hepatic lesions. No biliary ductal dilatation. The portal and hepatic veins appear patent.

Nephrectomy Bed / Right Kidney: Expected post-surgical changes are again noted in the right renal fossa following prior right radical nephrectomy. The renal bed shows stable postoperative fibrosis without discrete mass, new nodularity, or soft tissue thickening to suggest local tumor recurrence. The surgical clips are in expected position.

Left Kidney: The solitary left kidney appears grossly within normal limits. No hydronephrosis. No renal calculi. No new mass.

Adrenal Glands: Both adrenal glands are unremarkable. The right adrenal gland (preserved at nephrectomy) appears normal. No adrenal nodules.

Spleen: Normal in size and attenuation. No splenic lesions.

Pancreas: The pancreas enhances homogeneously without ductal dilatation, focal mass, or peripancreatic stranding.

Gastrointestinal Tract: The visualized bowel loops are unremarkable. No wall thickening, obstruction, or perforation. The appendix is visualized and appears normal.

Retroperitoneum: No pathologically enlarged retroperitoneal or mesenteric lymph nodes are identified. The aorta is normal caliber. No retroperitoneal fluid collection or hematoma.

PELVIS:

Osseous Pelvis: No lytic or sclerotic lesions suspicious for metastatic disease. Mild degenerative changes in the bilateral sacroiliac joints and hip joints, unchanged. No aggressive periosteal reaction.

Bladder: The urinary bladder is partially distended and appears unremarkable. No intraluminal mass.

Pelvic Soft Tissues: No pelvic lymphadenopathy. No free fluid.

SKELETON (visualized): No new osseous lesions suspicious for metastatic disease throughout the visualized axial and appendicular skeleton. Degenerative changes previously noted in the lumbar spine are unchanged. No vertebral body height loss to suggest pathologic compression fracture.

--- IMPRESSION ---

1. The right lower lobe dominant pulmonary nodule has decreased slightly in size compared to the pre-cabozantinib imaging, now approximately 1.9 cm compared to 2.4 cm. The remaining pulmonary nodules throughout both lungs are similarly modestly smaller or unchanged from prior. No new pulmonary nodules.

2. The two right hepatic lobe lesions remain stable in size and enhancement pattern compared to prior. No new hepatic lesions are identified.

3. No evidence of local recurrence at the right nephrectomy bed.

4. No new sites of disease in the chest, abdomen, or pelvis. No new lymphadenopathy.

5. No suspicious osseous lesions in the visualized skeleton.

Overall, the imaging findings reflect modest interval improvement in the dominant pulmonary lesions and stability of the hepatic disease compared to the pre-treatment baseline, consistent with partial disease control in the setting of ongoing cabozantinib therapy. The degree of change is modest and falls short of a clear-cut major response, though there has been meaningful improvement from the nadir of progression on the prior immunotherapy regimen.

Electronically signed by: [Attending Radiologist]
Division of Abdominal and Thoracic Imaging
